steps.observation:
In a shocking twist of events, Faith has been gruesomely murdered by a lawnmower in her own kitchen and now, Detective Winston must unravel the tangled web of suspicion entangling Clyde and Paul.

As Winston entered a well-kept home, he observed Clyde, an army man of some years, meticulously dusting and straightening up everything in sight. Checking his watch, Winston observed the room to be empty of people except for himself and Clyde. It was a known fact Clyde only did his cleaning when there was no one else in the room, a peculiar habit of his. "Evening, Clyde," he said by way of greeting.

"Detective," Clyde returned, his face neutral. His large lawn, recently mowed, was visible through the window. The neighbours had seen him mowing it just yesterday, a job required a heavy machine like a lawnmower which, due to its aggressiveness, demanded some level of foundational weapon training.

Winston gazed around the room, making a mental note of the pristine condition of the house. "You keep a fine home," Winston commented, to which Clyde merely offered a grunt of response and continued his clean-up. He knew the moment guests would arrive, he'd start cleaning again from scratch in his usual obsessive way.

Winston then recalled what some prior guests had mentioned, they noticed certain items of theirs were missing when they left Clyde's house. He quoted an instance, “Mr. Johnson, your neighbour across the street told me about his missing gloves after he visited your house last t...

- However, no direct evidence links Clyde to the crime scene or murder weapon.

His desperation for money and financial gain from her death makes him a prime suspect in her gruesome murder.

Mack is a designer with a snake hobby and a grudge against Clyde for threatening his sister. steps.observation:
In the tranquil solitude of a secluded chalet, Clyde meets an untimely death from snake venom; a case which sees the sharp mind of Detective Winston matching wits with two elusive suspects, Mack and Taylor.

As Winston walked into the sleek design studio, everything looked like it was out of a luxurious catalog. Mack, the man responsible for the rich interior designs was engrossed in sketching a new collection, his exotic reptile sculptures reflecting his extensive knowledge of reptile anatomy and he was known for keeping up with the latest snake discoveries as part of his hobby. 

“You Mack?” Winston asked, catching the man's attention. Mack turned to face the detective, revealing a chiseled face accentuated with some disturbing scars. 

"Mack's who you're chattin' to now." His tone was casual but assertive. His posture was confident, but his eyes, they held a disconcerting intensity, like a reptile about to strike. 

Winston was about to ask about the intriguing designs, instead, Mack strode across the room to a glass terrarium. He sifted his way through a mess of receipts on the barren desk to pull out a small, clear plastic box. 

"I've a hobby that keeps me distracted from work sometimes. Reptiles... snakes, to be precise. A passion I picked up in college," he said. He chuckled a little as he offered some snake food to the terrifying critter, its vibrant colors making one's blood run cold.

On noticing the detective's curiosity regarding the terrarium and the snake held ...

"Feeling okay?" He began with a simple question, hoping to ease her into the conversation.

Erika nodded meekly, "Yes, he was... threatening to expose my past…” her voice trailed off.

Shifting the topic, Winston questioned, "So, you two entered the arena together?"

"Yes, we did," Erika answered promptly, "Immediately, before the game started, I happened to notice a toolbox near the entrance. Strangely, there was an axe in it."

"You saw the axe?" Winston asked, eyebrows knitting together in surprise.

"I did," Erika answered, "I was a champion lumberjack, you know. A veteran of numerous competitions, exhibiting high axe-wielding skills. Never expected to see one in a place like this, though."

"A champion lumberj...
